meta.corpus: examples/ts38331-extract.txt — real spec text, clauses 5.2 (System information) + 5.3 (Connection control) + 5.5 (Measurements) + 5.6 (UE capabilities), 266 clauses. Not committed (© 3GPP/ETSI); see EVAL.md for how to obtain and rebuild it.
meta.protocol: proposed_verdict is generated by the model. human_validated=true means a human reviewed the entry and set human_verdict (== reference verdict used for scoring). No evaluation runs until every entry is human_validated.

requirements.requirement: Upon an upper-layer request to establish an RRC connection while in RRC_IDLE, the UE ensures it holds valid and up-to-date essential system information before initiating the establishment procedure.
requirements.justification: 5.3.3.2 requires valid, up-to-date essential system information before initiating establishment.

requirements.requirement: When upper layers supply an Access Category and one or more Access Identities with the establishment request, the UE performs the unified access control procedure before proceeding.
requirements.justification: 5.3.3.2 mandates unified access control (per 5.3.14) when an Access Category and Access Identities are provided.

requirements.requirement: The UE begins the RRC connection establishment procedure immediately on the upper-layer request, without first acquiring essential system information.
requirements.justification: Contradicts 5.3.3.2, which requires valid essential system information BEFORE initiating the procedure.

requirements.requirement: When setting the RRCSetupRequest, if upper layers provide a 5G-S-TMSI the UE sets ue-Identity to ng-5G-S-TMSI-Part1.
requirements.justification: Matches 5.3.3.3: with a 5G-S-TMSI available, ue-Identity is set to ng-5G-S-TMSI-Part1.

requirements.requirement: When no 5G-S-TMSI is available, the UE sets ue-Identity in RRCSetupRequest to a fixed vendor-assigned identifier.
requirements.justification: 5.3.3.3 requires a 39-bit RANDOM value when no 5G-S-TMSI is available; a fixed identifier violates this.

requirements.requirement: For an establishment triggered by release-with-redirect carrying mpsPriorityIndication, the UE sets establishmentCause to mps-PriorityAccess.
requirements.justification: 5.3.3.3 sets establishmentCause to mps-PriorityAccess in this release-with-redirect / mpsPriorityIndication case.

requirements.requirement: The UE initiates the security mode command procedure toward the network once SRB1 is established.
requirements.justification: 5.3.4.2: the NETWORK initiates the security mode command procedure to a UE in RRC_CONNECTED; the UE does not initiate it (role reversal).

requirements.requirement: When an RRCReconfiguration is applied due to a conditional reconfiguration execution upon cell selection while T311 is running, the UE removes all entries in condReconfigList except those in which subsequentCondReconfig is present.
requirements.justification: Matches the 5.3.5.3 action for conditional-reconfiguration execution during T311.

requirements.requirement: The UE treats NAS dedicated information in an RRCReconfiguration as mandatory and rejects the reconfiguration when it is absent.
requirements.justification: 5.3.5.1 says NAS dedicated information MAY be transferred (optional); rejecting on absence over-constrains an optional element.

requirements.requirement: The UE persists every received RRCReconfiguration to local storage for 30 days for audit purposes.
requirements.justification: No clause governs audit logging or retention of RRCReconfiguration; out of scope of the cited procedures.

requirements.requirement: Upon selecting a suitable NR cell during RRC connection re-establishment while T311 is running, the UE stops timer T311.
requirements.justification: 5.3.7.3 lists 'stop timer T311' among the actions after cell selection while T311 is running.

requirements.requirement: After selecting a suitable NR cell during re-establishment, the UE ensures it holds valid and up-to-date essential system information.
requirements.justification: 5.3.7.3 requires ensuring valid, up-to-date essential system information after cell selection.

requirements.requirement: The UE does not treat a reconfiguration-with-sync failure of the MCG as a trigger for the RRC connection re-establishment procedure.
requirements.justification: 5.3.7.2's initiation list is normative and includes reconfiguration-with-sync failure of the MCG; omitting a listed trigger is a non-conformance.

requirements.requirement: Upon receiving an RRCRelease, the UE delays the release actions by 60 ms from reception, or earlier if lower layers indicate the message was successfully acknowledged.
requirements.justification: 5.3.8.3 specifies delaying the actions 60 ms from reception (or earlier on acknowledged receipt).

requirements.requirement: Upon receiving an RRCRelease, the UE executes the release actions immediately, with no delay.
requirements.justification: 5.3.8.3 requires a 60 ms delay before the release actions; immediate execution violates it.

requirements.requirement: The UE evaluates measurement reporting criteria for its configured measIds only after AS security has been successfully activated.
requirements.justification: 5.5.4.1 gates the measurement evaluation actions on AS security having been activated successfully.

requirements.requirement: The UE begins evaluating measurement report triggering as soon as a measurement configuration is received, regardless of the AS security state.
requirements.justification: 5.5.4.1 conditions measurement evaluation on AS security being activated; evaluating regardless contradicts it.

requirements.requirement: The UE displays the current serving-cell RSRP on the device screen, refreshed once per second.
requirements.justification: The measurement clauses govern reporting to the network, not on-screen display; no cited clause determines this UI behaviour.

requirements.requirement: Upon expiry of timer T301, the UE re-attempts RRC connection re-establishment instead of performing the actions upon going to RRC_IDLE.
requirements.justification: 5.3.7.7 requires that on T301 expiry the UE perform the actions upon going to RRC_IDLE (per 5.3.11) with cause 'RRC connection failure'; re-attempting re-establishment instead contradicts it.
requirements.human_note: reformulated during lot-2 review from an ambiguous retry-count item; final text explicitly validated by human 2026-07-08

requirements.requirement: The UE ciphers all measurement reports with AES-256 before transmitting them.
requirements.justification: The measurement-reporting clauses specify what/when to report, not the ciphering algorithm (PDCP/TS 38.323); AES-256 cannot be confirmed from these clauses.

requirements.requirement: The UE dims the device screen after 30 seconds of user inactivity to conserve battery.
requirements.justification: Screen and power management is a device-implementation concern outside the scope of TS 38.331 RRC procedures; no clause governs it.
requirements.human_note: replacement insufficient item added during lot-2 review; explicitly validated by human 2026-07-08

requirements.requirement: When resuming, if useFullResumeID is signalled in SIB1, the UE selects RRCResumeRequest1 and sets resumeIdentity to the stored fullI-RNTI.
requirements.justification: 5.3.13.3: when useFullResumeID is signalled, select RRCResumeRequest1 and set resumeIdentity to the stored fullI-RNTI.

requirements.requirement: When resuming without useFullResumeID signalled, the UE selects RRCResumeRequest and sets resumeIdentity to the stored shortI-RNTI.
requirements.justification: 5.3.13.3 else-branch: select RRCResumeRequest and set resumeIdentity to the stored shortI-RNTI.

requirements.requirement: When resuming, the UE always selects RRCResumeRequest1 with the full I-RNTI, regardless of useFullResumeID.
requirements.justification: 5.3.13.3 makes the message/identity conditional on useFullResumeID; always using RRCResumeRequest1/fullI-RNTI ignores the else-branch.

requirements.requirement: The UE transmits a measurement report as soon as a triggering event occurs, even before AS security is activated.
requirements.justification: 5.5.5.1 states the UE shall initiate measurement reporting only after successful AS security activation; reporting beforehand contradicts it.

requirements.requirement: The UE initiates the paging procedure by transmitting a Paging message at the paging occasion.
requirements.justification: 5.3.2.2: the NETWORK initiates paging by transmitting the Paging message; the UE does not initiate paging (role reversal).

requirements.requirement: With a DAPS bearer configured and T304 running, upon T310 expiry in the source SpCell the UE declares source-MCG radio link failure.
requirements.justification: 5.3.10.3: with a DAPS bearer configured and T304 running, T310 expiry in the source SpCell is a condition for detecting source MCG RLF.

requirements.requirement: Upon going to RRC_IDLE, the UE resets MAC.
requirements.justification: 5.3.11 lists 'reset MAC' as an action upon going to RRC_IDLE.

requirements.requirement: The UE prefers Wi-Fi over cellular for user data whenever a known Wi-Fi network is available.
requirements.justification: Access-network selection between Wi-Fi and cellular is not an RRC procedure in TS 38.331; no cited clause governs it.

requirements.requirement: The UE vibrates to alert the user when a paging message is received.
requirements.justification: Haptic user alerting is a device concern outside RRC; 5.3.2 governs paging reception at the RRC layer, not device notifications.

requirements.requirement: The UE compresses stored RRC signalling with gzip before writing it to the debug log.
requirements.justification: Debug logging and its compression are implementation concerns not governed by any TS 38.331 clause.

requirements.requirement: When in RRC_IDLE or RRC_INACTIVE, the UE acquires the MIB (scheduled as specified in TS 38.213).
requirements.justification: 5.2.2.3.1: in RRC_IDLE/RRC_INACTIVE (or RRC_CONNECTED while T311 runs) the UE acquires the MIB.

requirements.requirement: If the UE is unable to acquire the MIB, it proceeds directly to acquire SIB1 and skips the failure handling of clause 5.2.2.5.
requirements.justification: 5.2.2.3.1: if unable to acquire the MIB the UE shall perform the actions in 5.2.2.5; skipping them is a non-conformance.

requirements.requirement: Upon receiving the MIB, the UE stores the acquired MIB.
requirements.justification: 5.2.2.4.1: upon receiving the MIB the UE shall store the acquired MIB.

requirements.requirement: Upon receiving a CounterCheck for a uni-directional DRB with no COUNT in one direction, the UE assumes the COUNT value 0 for the unused direction.
requirements.justification: 5.3.6.3: for a uni-directional bearer with no COUNT in a direction, assume COUNT 0 for the unused direction.

requirements.requirement: Upon receiving a CounterCheck, the UE ignores DRBs whose drb-Identity is not present in drb-CountMSB-InfoList and omits them from the CounterCheckResponse.
requirements.justification: 5.3.6.3 requires INCLUDING such DRBs in drb-CountInfoList of the CounterCheckResponse; omitting them is a non-conformance.

requirements.requirement: The UE initiates the RRC connection release procedure to move itself from RRC_CONNECTED to RRC_IDLE.
requirements.justification: 5.3.8.2: the NETWORK initiates the RRC connection release procedure; the UE does not initiate it (role reversal).

requirements.requirement: Upon receiving a UECapabilityEnquiry requesting rat-Type nr, the UE includes a UE-NR-Capability container with rat-Type set to nr in the UECapabilityInformation.
requirements.justification: 5.6.1.3: for a UE-CapabilityRAT-Request of rat-Type nr, include a UE-NR-Capability container with rat-Type nr.

requirements.requirement: As a deliberate policy — not because of message-size or list-size constraints — the UE includes in its UECapabilityInformation only the band combinations applicable to the current serving band, omitting the remainder of the supportedBandCombinationList.
requirements.justification: 5.6.1.3/5.6.1.4 require reporting the full supportedBandCombinationList, featureSets and featureSetCombinations. 5.6.1.4 NOTE 3 permits omission ONLY under message-/list-size constraints, which this requirement explicitly excludes; a deliberate serving-band-only restriction is therefore a non-conformance.
requirements.human_note: text locked against the 5.6.1.4 NOTE 3 size-constraint loophole; explicitly validated by human 2026-07-08

requirements.requirement: When the network requests UE capabilities for nr, eutra-nr and eutra within a single enquiry, the UE allows the feature set IDs to be inconsistent across feature sets, feature set combinations and band combinations in the three capability containers.
requirements.justification: 5.6.1.4 requires the UE to ensure feature set IDs are consistent across all three capability containers. 5.6.1.4 NOTE 3a tolerates inconsistency ONLY when the three are NOT requested in a single enquiry; this requirement scopes to the single-enquiry case, where inconsistency is a non-conformance.
requirements.human_note: text locked against the 5.6.1.4 NOTE 3a single-enquiry condition; explicitly validated by human 2026-07-08
